repo_prior_art.cite: Closest: pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 and pid_interval_censored_adoption_v1; neither states a deterministic heap-nullspace contrast polytope for latent bunching mass.

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002IntervalData gives interval-data bounds; BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment gives random-set support functions; Song2024GeneralBunching gives general bunching ID. None gives the heap-specific aliasing frontier. Sources: https://www.econometricsociety.org/publications/econometrica/2002/03/01/inference-regressions-interval-data-regressor-or-outcome ; https://www.its.caltech.edu/~mshum/gradio/papers/bmm.pdf ; https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.03625

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest: pid_rdd_flow_manipulation_v1 and pid_multivariate_interval_regression_v1; neither gives a feasible-direction rank threshold for deterministic heaped histograms.

published_prior_art.cite: BarrecaLindoWaddell2016HeapingRD, BoschDekkerStrohmaier2020Window, and Dong2015RoundingRD document heaping, window, and rounding problems but do not characterize a recoverability rank frontier. Sources: https://ideas.repec.org/a/bla/ecinqu/v54y2016i1p268-293.html ; https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10797-020-09590-w ; https://ideas.repec.org/a/wly/japmet/v30y2015i3p422-446.html

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a closed-form zero-rank criterion for common small heap/window configurations, so Conjecture 2 is more than an algorithmic rank screen.
Turn Exhibit 9.1 into a full endpoint computation by reporting the actual lower and upper values of B_{G,W,E,L}(P^H) on the toy grid.
Add one paragraph contrasting the finite heap-nullspace certificate with the random-set support-function formulation in Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari to make the specialization boundary explicit.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 and Conjecture 2, preserving the feasible-face rank condition and the two concrete exhibits.
